title: Frederick Douglass: Forever Free
titleZh: 弗雷德里克·道格拉斯：永远自由

q.en: How did young Frederick Douglass learn to read and write?
q.zh: 小时候的弗雷德里克·道格拉斯是如何学会读写的？

options.en: He traded bread to white boys who gave him lessons.
options.zh: 他用面包跟白人男孩换读写课。

options.en: He went to a regular school paid by his owner.
options.zh: 他上了主人付费的正规学校。

options.en: He taught himself from television.
A newspaper taught him automatically.

explain.en: Frederick traded bread for reading and writing lessons with boys who could go to school.
explain.zh: 弗雷德里克用面包向能上学的男孩换取读写课。

q.en: Why did Sophia Auld's husband make her stop teaching Frederick?
q.zh: 为什么索菲亚·奥尔德的丈夫让她停止教弗雷德里克？

options.en: He believed education and slavery were incompatible.
options.zh: 他认为受教育与当奴隶不能共存。

options.en: Frederick was too old to learn.
options.zh: 弗雷德里克年纪太大学不动了。

options.en: There were no books in the house.
Frederick did not want to learn.

explain.en: Mr. Auld said a slave who learned to read would want freedom, so education and slavery don't mix.
explain.zh: 奥尔德先生说会读写的奴隶会渴望自由，所以受教育与为奴不相容。

q.en: After reading about the abolition movement, what did Frederick understand?
q.zh: 读了废奴运动的相关内容后，弗雷德里克明白了什么？

options.en: That some white people also wanted to end slavery.
options.zh: 有些白人也想废除奴隶制。

options.en: That slavery would last forever.
That reading was illegal for everyone.
options.zh: 对所有人来说读书都违法。

options.en: That he should stop learning.

explain.en: He learned some white people wanted to abolish slavery too, and he began dreaming of escape.
explain.zh: 他得知有些白人也想废除奴隶制，于是开始梦想逃亡。

q.en: What does Douglass's line 'Once you learn to read, you will be forever free' suggest?
q.zh: 道格拉斯的话'一旦学会阅读，你就永远自由'说明了什么？

options.en: Education and freedom are closely linked.
Reading is a waste of time.
Only rich people can be free.
Freedom comes from money.

explain.en: The book connects understanding through reading with the desire and hope for freedom.
explain.zh: 书中把通过阅读获得的认知与对自由的渴望和希望联系在一起。

q.en: How did Frederick Douglass escape to freedom?
q.zh: 弗雷德里克·道格拉斯是如何逃到自由之地的？

options.en: Dressed as a sailor with a freedman's passport, through Delaware and Pennsylvania to New York.
options.zh: 扮成水手、拿着自由人通行证，经特拉华和宾州到达纽约。

options.en: He flew north in a hot-air balloon.
He won a contest that granted freedom.
options.zh: 他赢了一场比赛获得自由。

options.en: He was carried by a train secretly.

explain.en: Douglass traveled disguised as a sailor with a freedman's passport to New York.
explain.zh: 道格拉斯伪装成水手，拿着自由人通行证前往纽约。

options.en: A secret network with safe houses that helped fugitive slaves escape.
options.zh: 由安全屋组成的秘密网络，帮助逃亡奴隶。

options.en: A real train that ran underground.
options.zh: 一条真正在地下运行的火车。

options.en: A type of boat on rivers.
A school for slaves.

explain.en: Abolitionists organized safe houses and conductors to help runaway slaves reach freedom.
explain.zh: 废奴主义者组织安全屋和向导，帮助逃亡奴隶获得自由。

q.en: What famous speech did Douglass give on July 5, 1852?
q.zh: 道格拉斯在1852年7月5日发表了哪篇著名演讲？

options.en: 'What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July?'
options.zh: '奴隶的七月四日意味着什么？'

options.en: 'I Have a Dream'
'The Gettysburg Address'
'Give Me Liberty'

explain.en: In 1852 Douglass asked what Independence Day meant for slaves and former slaves.
explain.zh: 1852年道格拉斯质问独立日对奴隶和解放奴隶意味着什么。

q.en: Why does the author include many of Douglass's own quotes?
q.zh: 作者为什么要引用道格拉斯的许多原话？

options.en: To let readers hear his real voice and perspective.
options.zh: 让读者听到他真实的声音和视角。

options.en: To fill up the pages with text.
Because the author could not write.
To confuse the reader.

explain.en: Quotes show Douglass's eloquence and personal point of view directly.
explain.zh: 引文直接展现了道格拉斯的口才与亲身视角。

q.en: Which amendment gave Black men the right to vote in 1870?
q.zh: 哪项修正案在1870年赋予黑人男性投票权？

options.en: The Fifteenth Amendment.
The First Amendment.
The Tenth Amendment.
The Twentieth Amendment.

explain.en: The Fifteenth Amendment (1870) gave every male citizen, including former slaves, the right to vote.
explain.zh: 第十五修正案（1870年）赋予包括前奴隶在内的每位男性公民投票权。

q.en: How did Douglass help during the Civil War?
q.zh: 内战期间道格拉斯提供了什么帮助？

options.en: He advised Lincoln on the Emancipation Proclamation and pushed for Black soldiers.
options.zh: 他就《解放宣言》给林肯当顾问，并推动黑人参军。

options.en: He led the Confederate army.
He built the White House.
He wrote the Constitution.

explain.en: Douglass was Lincoln's advisor on the Emancipation Proclamation and urged Black soldiers to fight.
explain.zh: 道格拉斯是林肯《解放宣言》的顾问，并促成黑人入伍参战。

prompt.en: Douglass said 'Right is of no sex, truth is of no color.' What do you think he meant, and how does it connect to the rights of women and Black Americans?
prompt.zh: 道格拉斯说'权利不分性别，真理不分肤色'。你认为他是什么意思？这与妇女和非洲裔美国人的权利有何关联？

en: Think about his work for both ending slavery and women's right to vote.
Consider the idea that fairness should apply to everyone.
zh: 想想他既推动终结奴隶制，也支持妇女投票权。
想想公平应当适用于每一个人这一理念。
